options: The President's senior advisors who lead the executive departments
questions.explanation: The Cabinet is part of the executive branch, not a separate branch.

questions.q: How many executive departments are there?
How does a person become a Cabinet member?
options: The President nominates them and the Senate confirms most positions
By appointment of the Supreme Court
questions.explanation: This is another example of checks and balances.

questions.q: USCIS is part of which Cabinet department?
questions.explanation: USCIS is one of the agencies within the Department of Homeland Security.

questions.q: Who do Cabinet members report to?
questions.explanation: They report to the President; Congress provides oversight.
